message:
ci(workflows): improve reliability with timeouts, concurrency, and pinned actions

Apply best practices across all GitHub Actions workflows:

- Add concurrency groups to cancel redundant runs on push (except
  release/update-packages which should complete)
- Add timeout-minutes to all jobs to prevent hung workflows from
  consuming resources indefinitely
- Pin action versions for reproducibility:
  - ludeeus/action-shellcheck@master -> @2.0.0
  - softprops/action-gh-release@v2 -> @v2.2.1
  - peter-evans/create-pull-request@v5 -> @v7
- Use taiki-e/install-action for cargo-tarpaulin instead of building
  from source (faster CI)
- Replace EOL CentOS 7 with Rocky Linux 8 in musl distro tests

These changes reduce CI costs, improve build times, and increase
reproducibility of the pipeline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -7,10 +7,16 @@ on:
     branches: [main, master]
   workflow_dispatch:
 
+# Cancel in-progress runs when a new commit is pushed to the same branch
+concurrency:
+  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
+  cancel-in-progress: true
+
 jobs:
   version-check:
     name: Version Consistency
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 5
     steps:
       - name: Checkout
         uses: actions/checkout@v4
@@ -58,12 +64,13 @@ jobs:
   shellcheck:
     name: ShellCheck
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 5
     steps:
       - name: Checkout
         uses: actions/checkout@v4
 
       - name: Run ShellCheck
-        uses: ludeeus/action-shellcheck@master
+        uses: ludeeus/action-shellcheck@2.0.0
         with:
           severity: warning
           scandir: '.'
@@ -74,6 +81,7 @@ jobs:
